gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/187)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/187)
text: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا الْجَارُودِ يُحَدِّثُ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) قَالَ أَرْبَعَةٌ نَزَلَتْ مِنَ الْجَنَّةِ الْعِنَبُ الرَّازِقِيُّ وَ الرُّطَبُ الْمُشَانُ وَ الرُّمَّانُ الْإِمْلِيسِيُّ وَ التُّفَّاحُ الشَّيْسَقَانُ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Abdullah Bin Ja’far, from Abdul Aziz Bin Zakariyya Al Lu’lui, from Suleyman Bin Al Mufazzal who said,
‘I heard Abu Al-Jaroud narrating Abou Abu Ja’farasws having said: ‘Four descended from the Paradise – Al-Raziqy grape, and Al-Mushan date, and Al-Amleysi pomegranate, and Al-Sheysqan apple’.
en.sarwar: 2. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from ‘Abd Allah ibn Ja‘far from ‘Abd ‘Abd al-’Aziz ibn Zakariya al-Lu’lu’iy from Sulayman ibn al-Mufaddal who has said the following: “I heard abu al-Jarud narrate from abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, saying, ‘Four kinds of fruit came from paradise: There were grapes of al-Raziqiy, fresh dates of al-Mushan, pomegranates of al-Tmlisiy and apples of al-Shaysaqan.’”
